response.justification: Candidate is a strong generalist engineer with notable systems-level work in DLang (low-latency microservices at Sociomantic handling millions of requests/sec) and genuine geospatial domain exposure through GISCollective and Green Map. However, the role explicitly requires modern C++ (or Rust) as the primary stack, and the resume shows only ~6 months of C/C++ work at National Instruments back in 2014–2015 plus a bachelor-thesis project. No Rust experience, no routing/navigation tiling background, and recent work has been TypeScript/Node-centric. Worth a screening conversation only if the team is open to a DLang-to-C++ transition based on systems fundamentals.
strengths: Proven low-latency distributed systems experience in DLang (Sociomantic microservices, async I/O, fibers)
Direct geospatial/GIS domain background via GISCollective and Open Green Map
Strong engineering discipline: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, open-source contributions
concerns: C++ experience is minimal (~6 months in 2014–2015) and outdated; no Rust experience listed
No navigation, routing, or map-tiling pipeline experience
Recent 5+ years focused on TypeScript/Node web stacks rather than systems programming

key_factors.factor: Systems-level low-latency work in DLang transferable to C++/Rust
